description: My overall experience with Dropbox Paper has been positive. It offers a clean and intuitive interface that makes collaboration easy and efficient. The ability to work in real-time with others, coupled with its integration with Dropbox, streamlines project management. However, I wish it had more robust formatting tools to handle a wide range it documents.
author: Neelam R.
pros: Its seamless collaboration features, allowing real-time editing and brainstorming with my team, all in one beautifully designed workspace.
cons: less advanced formatting options especially when trying to create more complex documents or presentations
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

review_title: Simple, Clean, and Great for Teamwork
description: It’s been really solid for working with other people and keeping ideas organized. I’ve used it for lists, planning, and creative stuff, and it works great for that. It’s just not as powerful as Google Docs if you need all the bells and whistles
author: Kay R.
pros: I really like how clean and simple Dropbox Paper feels. It’s not cluttered like some other tools, so it’s super easy to just start writing or brainstorming. I also love that you can drop in videos, images, and even Spotify links – everything looks good and works smoothly
cons: It’s a bit basic when it comes to formatting. If you’re trying to make a super polished document, it’s not the best for that. Also, you can’t really use it offline, which can be annoying when you don’t have great internet.

author: Blake D.
pros: I love Dropbox for the “easy to use” feels sometimes, depending on what you’re doing and it gives you lots of space for any type plod project you may have or files and documents
cons: It can sometimes be where it’s too much going on if you’re not use to it. Once you get use to it then it’s perfect!

description: So far my experience has been well. I like the collaboration and organization features that are offered.
author: Autumn B.
pros: I love the organization features when it comes to to-do list for the team and due dates
cons: I’m still new to it so I’m maneuver through, no cons yet
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: So simple, but very power for organization and task coordination
description: Paper is, for my team, the ideal replacement for hardcopy notes and simple assignments. Too many alternatives try doing too much in a small window of use. Dropbox Paper keeps you connected, on the same page, and mobile when need be.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: The most powerful feature for us (working in software) has been the simplistic yet powerful markup and shortcuts system. The formatting is so easy to use but also isn't restricted to one method, allowing more mouse-centric or keyboard-oriented users flexability.
cons: I wouldn't mind seeing a dedicated desktop application in addition to the browser interface. Even if it was just a PWA, it would keep users from being so married to more tabs in their web browser.

description: as a new manager in this company (school) I have introduced Dropbox to improve the way all work processes and to track all the different operations tasks and projects. so it solved so many problems related to managing and tracking projects and it yielded good results
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Dropbox has been round for quite sometime. it has many advantages:Apps available for practically every operating systemFlexible array of collaborative toolsSolid offerings for premium subscribersDigital signatures enabledSuperb app integrationAutomatic file backup and synchronizationOffline functionalityit is still one of the best apps out there
cons: however it needs some improvementsPoor storage limits for free usersComparatively expensive paid subscriptionsSecurity weaknessesLimited search functionif these elements were improved, the app will be the best in the market

description: I work on an IT team, and I've started using Dropbox Paper's to-do list features to create my information technology plans. However, I am hoping it had a daily calendar alternative! Overall, it's a valuable method for managing projects when working with others.
author: Habibah M.
pros: I believe Dropbox Paper is a relatively new feature. It has a fantastic general layout that allows you to create tables, describe the text, create a to-do list, tag people, and create a timeline. You may emphasize text or leave a note directly in the paper, making it simple to keep track of progress and make decisions.
cons: I hope the to-do list could place the dates in sequential sequence and attach them to the calendar automatically. The choice is currently available, but it is entirely manual. Plus, for color coding, I need more highlight color choices so it i can distinguished between the notes or the editing that has added to the document.

review_title: Great tool for writing and sharing
description: I love this tool. I write practically all my text with it.
author: Javier A.
pros: Paper is a incredibly well polished cloud based wysiwyg markdown editor and along with Medium has set the standard for these. It includes some handy features like tables and to-do lists It comes directly with your Dropbox account. It also has very easy sharing. But above all its minimalistic interface helps you focus on writing and removes most distractions.
cons: Since it is markdown based, it lacks but the most basic formatting options. So if you want more visual adjustments or advanced word processing features, you won't find them in paper

description: at the end this this helps in a great way people to share and create works really great.
author: alex M.
pros: it gave me a lot of option of creations tools to work with and it works good for what i need.
cons: some times its really difficult to understand sometime the way it is and complicate .
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

description: It’s efficient and stores all documents needs for my position.
author: Brittany P.
pros: That it is efficient for jobs that are work from home but still need to access documents from the company.
cons: No dislikes at this time. It has been a benefit to us right now.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: A lot of features for as simple as easy as it is.
description: I don't use it as much as other note-capturing apps (Like Apple Notes and google docs), but I have been impressed with how easy DropBox Paper is and the features it has.
author: Grant H.
pros: The features, especially: Timeline, tables, ToDo lists, adding media.
cons: It is not quite as quick or convenient as Apple Notes or services like that.

review_title: Storing and sharing of files in Dropbox
description: Designing a simpler approach to keep files in sync was part of making work better for individuals. Today, it entails creating solutions that decrease busywork so you may concentrate on the important tasks. The majority of productivity tools obstruct your progress. They ding, distract, and disturb the flow of your team, so you spend your days hopping between apps and tracking down feedback. It's a waste of time, not the important stuff. This is something we want to fix. I believe that there is a better way to work. Dropbox makes it easier for people to stay organized, stay focused, and communicate with their teams.
author: Arlene G.
pros: Dropbox's key benefit is that it is absolutely free. There are no hidden fees or extra expenses after you begin using the service. When you sign up for a Dropbox account, you get two gigabytes (GB) of storage space for free. This is a generous amount of storage. There are, however, ways to expand your area without spending a dime. Sending Dropbox fan mail, connecting your Dropbox to social media, and urging family, friends, and other individuals to join Dropbox are all examples of these ways. Dropbox allows you to share files. All you have to do is place the files in a folder that is designated for sharing with certain users or the general public to do this. Dropbox is also an excellent platform for storing and backing up data. This implies that if your computer or smartphone fails or is lost, you can simply access all of the files stored on it using Dropbox. This will save you a lot of time and effort.
cons: The security of the files is one of the tool's drawbacks. Dropbox's security has been called into question. There have been a number of instances of servers being hacked and data being stolen. As a result, any sensitive information stored on Dropbox should be kept safe. The encryption used to protect user data on Dropbox is currently only known by the developers. Users are not permitted to encrypt their information before uploading it to Dropbox.

description: Dropbox paper is great when collaborating as a team.
author: Becky M.
pros: I use Dropbox paper for meeting notes, project plans, event plans, and other miscellaneous documents. The integration with Dropbox makes it a good option for teams and collaboration because it's easy to safely and securely share documents. It's easy to organize, format, and edit documents as well.Now I can turn any document into a template on Dropbox paper. That's a big deal because I can quickly get the exact document that I need and use it anytime. Just click the "Templatize" icon. It will be shareable as well. That's so much simpler than the old method of clearing information and re-entering.
cons: Dropbox is a little more expensive than it's competitors. Additionally, I like the security of sharing with Dropbox, however sometimes I need to share with someone who does not have a Dropbox account and I want to drop those security measures for that particular instance. It's difficult to do that.

author: Robert W.
pros: Was super easy to get used to using and the ways to get items to team members
cons: Didnt see any cons to the program to write about

description: We use Dropbox paper when we need input from different team members working from home. We can share a task and work from the same platform to achieve a common goal. Dropbox Paper has a simple user interface so everyone gets started in the right direction. Everyone can see what is happening on the paper regardless of their location. However, internet connection problems stops everything. I also think it is relatively expensive.
author: Mwangi J.
pros: Dropbox paper allows team members to complete tasks from different locations. It stands out because everyone can perform a task which will reflect immediately on the platform. You can add text, files, or images required to complete a task. You can use files stored in your PC or Dropbox and it will reflect almost instantly especially if you have a reliable internet connection. It allows users to comment, share opinion, or leave feedback minimizing the need to hold online meetings, call, or send emails to each other.
cons: If you lose internet connection sometimes it takes time to start responding. I usually have to reload it to enjoy real-time activities. It has limited formatting features but offers better collaboration functions. The pricing could hinder potential clients.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

author: Pankaj K.
pros: Best feature of this software is easy to to use
cons: Sometimes we assigned lots of task which gives lots of notifications

description: I've used Paper as a personal and professional note-taking app for the past few years. I see it as mainly a competitor to Evernote and Confluence, however it is superior to both in my opinion. The collaboration tools are excellent and the 'to-do' list is sufficiently powerful for the majority of use-cases. For myself, the best features are the fact that it uses normal markdown syntax and supports latex equations. All you need is a web-browser to use it and the mobile app is very solid. The app is simple, but it makes excellent design choices and as a result, anyone can use it immediately. Low barrier-to-entry and simplicity are exactly what I want from a note-taking/collaboration tool, so I would highly recommend it. It's not a replacement for a word processor, nor does it intend to be.
author: Bram E.
pros: Simplicity, markdown syntax, latex markup, web content embedding, cross-platform
cons: I would like footnotes and references. Performance stutters with very large documents.

description: The whole office has access to it and it is user-friendly in my opinion
author: Danielle S.
pros: We are able to attach multiple documents to send to different parties or inter-office.
cons: Nothing. We love it and everyone has access to it.

review_title: Great for quick notes - will be there any future development?
description: Dropbox Paper is a quite good piece of software that helps me to be organised on my trips and share notes with my friends. I feel, however, that its development is a little bit in stagnation. I wish they could devote more care to this - especially if they could somehow integrate it into main desktop app.
author: Jan O.
pros: I really like how quickly I can make notes and share them, ingegrate images or even Google Maps. I am using many different types of software for notetaking but I happen to come back to Dropbox Paper every time I want to make a special document for one of my trips (research or leisure) with detailed timetable, pictures of tickets, ideas etc. It is also easy to share a very user friendly.
cons: My biggest issue is that it crashes on my Android phone (Nexus 6p). Otherwise, I think that it should be better integrated with the desktop Dropbox application. I really don't like that I have to always go on the website just to open the document and that Dropbox paper has a separate folder strcuture. It would be great if it would be simply another document in my Dropbox folder.

author: İbrahim A.
pros: I was amazed by the ability to export and embed code fragments into Markdown. I already had a dropbox account, which causes me to save my little notes in it. the interface is awesome. I recommend it to those who use many different os during the day, those who already store their files in dropbox.
cons: Desktop application is still not offered to business users. That's the only problem.

review_title: the easiest-to-use collaborative document creation platform
author: joel K.
pros: Real-time remote collaboration of a team of users, with the possibility of suggestions, comments and modifications
cons: I think that compared to other word processing tools, Paper may lack some advanced features such as more complex layout options and more detailed tracking of changes.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

author: Toyin B.
pros: Drop box is a great product i love it. Its compatible with all my other devices and its fast
cons: Honestly i cant really think of any negative points its a great product with lots of space

author: sheza H.
pros: It is easy to install on your computer/laptop and quite easy to use. Dropbox Paper is an excellent way to share the files. You can sync easily across all the connected devices.
cons: Sometimes, it becomes unresponsive, and uploading takes a lot of time. Other than that, I found it perfect.

author: Danilo C.
pros: It is a simple tool and offer a good way of storing my files
cons: They could offer more free space in the free version

author: Francielli B.
pros: I use dropbox for years with university, I find this app extremely reliable, I have it shared with other professional colleagues and I never had any problems. Always very fast to upload documents, I like the link between different devices and I highly recommend using it.
cons: The layout has improved a lot in the last few years, I would just like the document folders that had modifications to be in a different color, it would be easy to see.
